visa_types.name: Official visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/vnm/en/che/travel-insurance)
Document required for Official passport visa please click here. 
Please note as per instructions received from the Consulate of Switzerland, personal Bank statement (last 3 months) with stamp/signature of Bank in original needs to be submitted on A4 size only.
Please note that confirmation of residence mentioned on given checklist is mandatory required for application submission. The other documents might be used to replace would be unacceptable for consideration from Switzerland Consulate General.
“Applicants and travel agents are requested not to bind two or more passports with a stapler to avoid getting it damaged. Instead you may bind them together using rubber bands.”
No other format will be accepted at the VFS Global counter.
Please be noted that all documents must be translated into English/ French/ Italian/ German in A4 paper within 6 months
visa_types.visa_fees:
Holder of diplomatic and service passport are exempted of visa fee.
- Each case is reviewed separately at the time of application, and no guarantee can be given that a visa will be issued. In any case, the visa fees will be not refunded.
VFS Global Service Charges: Applicants shall be levied a service charge of VND 581.000 (All VFS’s service charges are inclusive taxes)
Please note:
- All fees are non-refundable.
- An official receipt should be obtained for all payments made.
visa_types.processing_time:
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 15 days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. 
Important: It is recommended to submit your visa application at least three to four weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period.
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
You are going to Switzerland on an official visit.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
You are going to Switzerland on an official visit.

## Visa Fees
Holder of diplomatic and service passport are exempted of visa fee.
- Each case is reviewed separately at the time of application, and no guarantee can be given that a visa will be issued. In any case, the visa fees will be not refunded.
VFS Global Service Charges: Applicants shall be levied a service charge of VND 581.000 (All VFS’s service charges are inclusive taxes)
Please note:
- All fees are non-refundable.
- An official receipt should be obtained for all payments made.

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/vnm/en/che/travel-insurance)
Document required for Official passport visa please click here. 
Please note as per instructions received from the Consulate of Switzerland, personal Bank statement (last 3 months) with stamp/signature of Bank in original needs to be submitted on A4 size only.
Please note that confirmation of residence mentioned on given checklist is mandatory required for application submission. The other documents might be used to replace would be unacceptable for consideration from Switzerland Consulate General.
“Applicants and travel agents are requested not to bind two or more passports with a stapler to avoid getting it damaged. Instead you may bind them together using rubber bands.”
No other format will be accepted at the VFS Global counter.
Please be noted that all documents must be translated into English/ French/ Italian/ German in A4 paper within 6 months 

## Photo Specifications
Note: Kindly be informed that we cannot accept photographs that do not meet below requirements.
Please provide one recent passport size picture, not older than 6 months (size 35 – 45 mm in width, no copy or scanned picture). The photographs should be in color and:
- Taken against a light background (white or off-white) so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear share quality and with the face in focus.
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print).
- Full face, non-smiling [without sunglasses, a hat/cap or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background].
- Please stick the Photograph on the Visa Application Form.
For more details, please refer to:http://www.icao.int/Security/mrtd/Downloads/Technical%20Reports/Annex_A-Photograph_Guidelines.pdf (https://www.icao.int/Security/mrtd/Downloads/Technical%20Reports/Annex_A-Photograph_Guidelines.pdf)
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete. Photo booth meeting these requirements is available at the centre.

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 15 days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. 
Important: It is recommended to submit your visa application at least three to four weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period.
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

## Application Form
Applicants who wish to obtain a Switzerland visa application form, can download the requisite form here.
Please note the instructions mentioned below while taking the printout.
Visa Application Form
Application form (German-English) 
Application form (French-English) 
Please note:
- Number of pages: 4
- You can also obtain this form from the visa application centre.
- It is mandatory to be filled by all applicants along with the application form.
To download a form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR 
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

Overview
Please note that applications for National Visa can only be submitted in person through the Consulate General of Switzerland in Ho Chi Minh City. Appointments are to be made in advance with the Consulate General by telephone on:
Phone: +84 (0) 28 62991200, Monday-Thursday, 2.00-3.30 p.m.
After the appointment, the Consulate General will forward the application to the competent cantonal authorities in Switzerland for a decision. The Consulate General of Switzerland can only issue the visa after approval by the competent authorities.
Information to the National Visa can be found on the Website (https://www.eda.admin.ch/countries/vietnam/en/home/visa/entry-ch/more-90-days.html) of the Consulate General.

Ho Chi Minh City 71006

visa_types.name: National Visa
visa_types.overview:
If you  plan to enter to Switzerland for a stay of more than 90 days, you need a  National Visa (visa D). National visa entitles you to stay up to 90 days,  within the validity of Visa D, to enter other Schengen countries. 
 Any  stay longer than 90 days falls under National jurisdiction and is not part of  the Schengen Agreement. 
      There  are different types of long stay visas: 
- Student  visa (more than 90 days) (#studies(morethan90days))
- Family  reunification. (#familyreunionforchildren/marriageandpartnershipregistrationinswitzerland)
- Work Permit
Please note that applications for National Visa can only be submitted in person through the Consulate General of Switzerland in Ho Chi Minh City. Appointments are to be made in advance with the Consulate General by telephone on:
Phone: +84 (0) 28 62991200, Monday-Thursday, 2.00-3.30 p.m.
After the appointment, the Consulate General will forward the application to the competent cantonal authorities in Switzerland for a decision. The Consulate General of Switzerland can only issue the visa after approval by the competent authorities.
Information to the National Visa can be found on the Website (https://www.eda.admin.ch/countries/vietnam/en/home/visa/entry-ch/more-90-days.html) of the Consulate General.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Vietnamese citizens and foreigners living in Vietnam with a valid residence/work permit travelling to Switzerland for gainful activities of more than 8 but less than 120 working days per calendar year
Any gainful activity is subject to authorization by the competent, cantonal migration authority. The employer must take the necessary steps in Switzerland and apply for the work permit of the visa applicant at the competent migration authority in the Canton where the working place is located beforehand. If the gainful activity is exercised for more than 120 days per calendar year, please refer to the relevant information under "National visa for a stay of more than 90 days" (https://www.eda.admin.ch/countries/vietnam/en/home/visa/entry-ch/more-90-days.html).

visa_types.name: Schengen Visa
visa_types.overview:
Short Stay visa entitles an applicant, who does not hold a residence permit, to stay in Switzerland for an uninterrupted period or a total of successive periods of stays that does not exceed 90 days in every six months since the date of first entry. Switzerland is one of the 29 European countries forming the Schengen Area which have no border controls between them.
 In case of transit visas, the duration is authorized in accordance with the period of stay required for the transit.
